response: Eleutherococcus is a genus of thorny shrubs and trees in the family Araliaceae, native to eastern Asia, including Japan, Southeast Siberia, and the Philippines. It includes 38 species, and one of its members, Eleutherococcus senticosus, is known as Siberian ginseng and contains compounds like isofraxidin-7-glucoside and eleutherosides. Amaryllis is a genus of flowering bulbs in the subtribe Amaryllidinae, characterized by two species, with "Amaryllis belladonna" being a native of South Africa. It is also associated with the common name "lily" due to its flower shape and growth habit, though it is distantly related to true lilies.
